title: Stress Melt 🌆 Raag Yaman Kalyan 174Hz | Sitar & Bansuri for Evening Rest
title_options: When Your Day's Weight Lingers 🌅 Raag Yaman Kalyan 174Hz | Sitar, Bansuri for Stress Detox
Nervous System Calm 🧘‍♀️ Raag Yaman Kalyan 174Hz | Sitar, Bansuri for Cortisol Drop
Dusk Unwinding 🌆 Raag Yaman Kalyan 174Hz | Sitar, Bansuri for Stress Relief
description:
Raag Yaman Kalyan 174Hz for evening cortisol drop. Sitar & Bansuri stress relief meditation music.

Lower your cortisol. Slow your breath. Let this raga do the rest.

0:00 Introduction — Setting the Intention
5:00 Alap — Free Exploration of Raag Yaman Kalyan
15:00 Vilambit — Slow & Deep Meditation
35:00 Madhya Laya — Deepening the Stillness
52:00 Samapti — Resolution & Inner Peace
58:00 Outro — Carry the Calm Forward

Raag Yaman Kalyan, a gem of Indian classical music, is perfect for the gentle unwinding of dusk. Its romantic and serene melodies are designed to soothe the nervous system, encouraging the mind and body to release the day's tension. The 174Hz frequency provides a foundational resonance for deep relaxation and a sense of emotional security, helping to gently ease discomfort and stress.

🧘‍♀️ PERFECT FOR:
- Evening unwind after a demanding day
- Releasing post-work stress and tension
- Preparing the mind for restorative sleep
- Supporting natural cortisol drop
- Gentle emotional detox before nightfall
- Cultivating a profound sense of inner peace

🎵 WHY RAAG YAMAN KALYAN HEALS:
This raga's melodic structure naturally guides the mind towards a parasympathetic state, activating the body's 'rest and digest' response. Its subtle intonations are known to harmonize energy centers, promoting emotional balance and a deep sense of calm.

────────────────────────────

🇮🇳 हिंदी में:
शाम के तनाव और दिन भर की थकान से मुक्ति के लिए यह राग यमन कल्याण 174Hz की सितार और बांसुरी धुन सुनें। अपने कोर्टिसोल के स्तर को कम करें और आंतरिक शांति का अनुभव करें।
"अपने कोर्टिसोल को कम करें। अपनी सांसों को धीमा करें। इस राग को बाकी काम करने दें।"

────────────────────────────

💛 If this music brought you stillness, please LIKE, SUBSCRIBE, and SHARE with someone who needs peace.

chapters.title: Alap — Free Exploration of Raag Yaman Kalyan

music_prompt: Instruments: Sitar and Bansuri. Raga: Yaman Kalyan. BPM: 40-45 (for meditative flow), with a very light, almost felt, Teentaal pulse at 80 BPM, emphasizing the space between bols, not the speed. Each note decays with long reverb, creating wide silence between phrases. There is no buildup, no climax, maintaining a consistently calm and introspective mood. The recording should have a warm, analog quality, designed for a deeply meditative and loop-friendly structure. The sitar will set the melodic foundation with warm meend (glides) and a gentle, expansive alap-style exploration of Yaman Kalyan's delicate notes, focusing on the komal notes that lend its romantic feel. The bansuri flute will engage in a gentle call-and-response, answering with breathy, long, sustained tones that feel like a slow exhale, melting into the sitar's phrases. The tabla will enter subtly with a light, sparse Teentaal, prioritizing resonance and space over rhythm. The overall playing feel should evoke relief, soft joy, and the sensation of the body unclenching after a long day. Exclude any Western instruments, percussion fills, or sudden dynamic shifts.
image_prompt: A richly textured Indian painting, blending Mughal miniature refinement with the vibrant, narrative detail of Madhubani folk art. The scene depicts a serene lakeside at dusk, rendered with warm earthy pigments and intricate crosshatching on aged parchment. Two musicians, a sitar player and a bansuri player, are seated opposite each other on a plush, lotus-adorned carpet that floats gently near the water's edge. Their eyes are closed in deep focus, their faces serene. Above them, a lush mango tree canopy, heavy with ripe fruit, frames the scene. Diyas (oil lamps) reflect their soft, flickering light in the perfectly still water of the lake. Peacocks preen gracefully on the shore, and a pair of deer graze peacefully in the golden light filtering through the trees. The sky transitions from a soft amber and crimson near the horizon to a deep peacock teal overhead, signaling the dissolution of day. Visible musical wisps, like gentle incense smoke or soft light rays, flow from the sitar and bansuri, creating delicate ripples on the water and softly illuminating the surrounding nature. The central figures are enveloped in a warm golden halo, emphasizing their tranquility. An ornate botanical border, featuring lotus and paisley motifs, frames the entire artwork.
video_prompt:
Animate this image as a seamless loop video.
Camera completely static and locked, no movement.

Only animate these subtle elements:
- Diyas flames (5% gentle flicker)
- Water surface with subtle ripples (3% slow movement)
- Sky glow transition from amber to teal (2% slow, gentle shift)
- Mango tree leaves with a very soft, barely perceptible rustle (3% subtle movement)
- Music wisps subtly drifting from instruments (80% slow, ethereal flow)

Everything else completely frozen and still:
sitar player, bansuri player, sitar, bansuri, lotus-adorned carpet, lotus flowers, mango tree branches, peacocks, deer, shoreline, ornate botanical borders, painted texture of the entire scene.

First frame and last frame must be identical.
No brightness change. No color shift. No flicker.
8 second seamless loop.
Mughal miniature meets regional folk art style preserved exactly.
A serene, painterly dusk scene where the world gently unclamps to the music.
